text: Writing clean code is like writing a clear message that anyone can understand. First, use proper naming conventions—variables like `userAge` or `orderTotal` are much better than `x` or `y`. This makes your code readable without extra comments. Second, break your program into small functions that each do one specific thing. For example, a function called `calculatePrice()` is easier to test and reuse than a long block of instructions. When you organise code this way, it becomes easier to maintain and debug in the future. Clean code saves time, reduces mistakes, and helps you collaborate more effectively with other developers.
